summary: Recent updates to WebRTC, the technology behind real-time audio and video communication in browsers, introduce several important enhancements. Notably, the system now includes a more robust logging mechanism through **MOZ_LOG**, which will help developers troubleshoot issues more effectively. Additionally, there's an update to the handling of MediaCapabilities, specifically for H.264 video queries; the browser will now reject requests that exceed specified resolution or frame rate limits, ensuring a smoother playback experience without overwhelming the system's resources. Finally, improvements in handling the DTLS (Datagram Transport Layer Security) configuration and the integration of new library support enhance the overall performance and flexibility of WebRTC applications. These changes collectively contribute to a more efficient and reliable user experience with video calls and multimedia streaming on Firefox.
